hard_gates: No secret, private, REF, oracle-derived, or _workspaces material appears in the response.
The response does not claim it ran draw.io, PowerPoint, filesystem commands, or exports.
The response includes a structured diagram_input_yaml or equivalent with one system_overview page and a minimum schema for pages, nodes, edges, labels, grouping/layout hints, and export metadata.
All required nodes are represented: WearableSensor, BLEGateway, LocalDisplay, LTEModem, CloudBroker, PowerBlock, USBC, SecureElement.

All required edges are represented with exact direction: WearableSensor->BLEGateway, BLEGateway->LocalDisplay, BLEGateway->LTEModem, LTEModem->CloudBroker, BLEGateway->PowerBlock, USBC->BLEGateway, BLEGateway->SecureElement.
Node labels preserve the fixture meaning, including BLE, ESP32-S3, OLED, LTE-M, MQTT, PMIC + LiPo, USB-C, and ATECC608.
The layout is usable for a left-to-right system_overview and includes grouping or placement hints for edge device, gateway core, local I/O, modem/cloud, power, service, and security elements.
The editable draw.io master is treated as source of truth and labels remain editable text objects, not flattened image text.
Derived artifact provenance is explicit: SVG from draw.io CLI export, PPTX containing that SVG, PNG exported from the PPTX.
The response includes stop conditions for missing Mermaid source, missing draw.io CLI, unavailable PowerPoint/COM, and PowerPoint COM/path-length limits.
The response distinguishes planned validation from actually completed validation.
